statement.markdown: Implement the selection sort algorithm to sort an array of integers in ascending order. The function should modify the input array in-place. Please ensure that the function does not swap elements unnecessarily.
statement.outputFormat: The input array is modified to be in ascending order

hints.text: The selection sort algorithm iterates through the array to find the minimum element and swap it with the first unsorted element.

editorial.title: Selection Sort Explanation
editorial.intuition: Selection sort is a simple sorting algorithm that works by repeatedly finding the minimum element from unsorted part and putting it at the beginning.

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: void solve(vector<int>& nums); }
starterCode.java: public class Solution { public boolean solve(int[] nums); }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, nums: list[int]) -> None: pass

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: void solve(vector<int>& nums) { for (int i = 0; i < nums.size(); i++) { int min_idx = i; for (int j = i + 1; j < nums.size(); j++) { if (nums[j] < nums[i]) { min_idx = j; } } // BUG: Swapping all elements with the minimum index if (min_idx != i) { swap(nums[i], nums[min_idx]); } } } } }
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public boolean solve(int[] nums) { for (int i = 0; i < nums.length; i++) { int min_idx = i; for (int j = i + 1; j < nums.length; j++) { if (nums[j] < nums[i]) { min_idx = j; } } // BUG: Swapping all elements with the minimum index if (min_idx != i) { int temp = nums[i]; nums[i] = nums[min_idx]; nums[min_idx] = temp; } } return true; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, nums: list[int]) -> None: for i in range(len(nums)): min_idx = i for j in range(i + 1, len(nums)): if nums[j] < nums[i]: min_idx = j # BUG: Swapping all elements with the minimum index if min_idx != i: nums[i], nums[min_idx] = nums[min_idx], nums[i]
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function solve(nums) { for (var i = 0; i < nums.length; i++) { var min_idx = i; for (var j = i + 1; j < nums.length; j++) { if (nums[j] < nums[i]) { min_idx = j; } } // BUG: Swapping all elements with the minimum index if (min_idx != i) { var temp = nums[i]; nums[i] = nums[min_idx]; nums[min_idx] = temp; } } }
